what_it_does: This file is a structured design-system audit and pattern inventory of a 30-page investor pitch deck for ConceptV/Counterpart, capturing slide-level narrative arcs, attention-flow descriptions, visual-design recipes, reusable component definitions, and content-length samples.
observations: Covers slides p18–p34, described as the appendix and a second embedded pitch arc
Contains detailed 'findings' organized by category: gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence
Each finding includes a name, recipe, evidence tied to slide numbers, an 'emergent' boolean, and a generator rule
Lists reusable components such as StatStrip, RoundedRectCard, InnovationCard, CircleMedia, TeamBioCard, FrostedContactCard, WorkflowStageConnector, CaseStudyZone, BottomBarSummary
Provides content_samples with character, word, and line counts for common text slots
Distinguishes two narrative registers: the primary raise story (p18–p20) and a self-contained second deck embedded as appendix content (p31–p34)
Uses a warm gold-and-cream palette with semantic color restrictions (green/red reserved for directional market indicators)
standouts: The appendix is treated not as supplementary material but as a second, self-contained pitch deck
Arrow direction is used as a semantic signal: right means forward/initiative, left means outcome/evidence
A frosted white card overlay is the prescribed mechanism for text on full-bleed photos, rather than photo dimming
Equal-gutter N-column grids are described as mathematically computed, not optical
A ghosted architectural line-drawing texture sits behind content slides at very low opacity
conventions: Follows a consistent JSON schema with category/name/recipe/evidence/emergent/generator_rule per finding
Provides empirical measurements: pixel sizes, hex colors, padding values, character counts, line limits
Ties every pattern to specific slide numbers as evidence
Maintains a restrained vocabulary: gold = brand accent, olive-gold = photo labels, cream = card fills, hatched-cream = data bands
concerns: Some categories overlap or duplicate concepts, e.g., the dashed connector appears under both 'geometry' and 'line-connector'
Color hex values are presented as precise but may be inferred/approximated from raster images without calibration metadata
The stated card body limit is 3 lines, yet the content_samples include a 5-line innovation card body (220 chars / 40 words / 5 lines)
The 'emergent' boolean is used without an explicit definition in this file
Highly prescriptive pixel rules may not transfer to different slide dimensions or output formats
notes: A dense, slide-by-slide design grammar extraction that treats a pitch deck's visual choices as reproducible components with pixel-level recipes and attention-flow maps.
questions: What exact slide dimensions and source image resolution were used to derive pixel measurements?
How were color hex values calibrated—by sampling, by brand guide, or by approximation?
What does the 'emergent' boolean precisely signify when classifying a finding?
Was the 5-line innovation card body on p34 an intentional exception to the 3-4 line body rule?
Are the QR codes shown in the case-study photos actually scannable links or illustrative placeholders?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the appendix and alternate-entry section of a 30-page pitch deck as a design grammar; the file does exactly that by cataloguing narrative arcs, visual recipes, components, and content samples, so purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Warm luxury aesthetic anchored by gold, cream, and architectural photography
Mathematical grid rigor and equal-column layouts
Semantic micro-typography where small details like arrow direction carry meaning
Appendix as an alternate narrative entry point
Generous whitespace as a structural device
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to turn a pitch deck's visual design into a reproducible, rule-based generator specification so that future slides or decks can be synthesized with the same look, feel, and rhetorical structure.
novelty: Distinctive elements include treating an investor pitch deck as a formal grammar with attention-flow maps, embedding a complete second pitch inside the appendix, using arrow direction as a semantic syntax, and prescribing a ghosted architectural floor-plan texture as a background constant.
contribution: This file contributes empirical visual recipes (frosted-card overlays, hatched stat strips, computed equal-gutter grids, semantic arrow rules), a reusable component library, content-length budgets, and the structural insight that appendix slides can form a standalone sub-deck—material a fused system would keep as concrete design rules.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-system extraction of slides 18–34 of a ConceptV/Counterpart investor pitch deck, cataloguing narrative arcs, attention flows, visual recipes, reusable UI components, and content-length samples.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the deck's visual grammar as reproducible generator rules and a component library for consistent slide production.
